tool.name: pepstats
tool.summary: Report deterministic protein summary statistics for sequence records

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `pepstats` reports a conservative first-release set of protein statistics for each input record. The Epithema v1 implementation includes raw sequence length, residue length excluding stop symbols, stop-count, amino-acid composition counts and frequencies, and a deterministic average-residue molecular-weight estimate.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local protein input path. Inputs are loaded through the shared Epithema readers for FASTA, FASTQ, EMBL, and GenBank. Nucleotide inputs are rejected.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits a stable table report with columns `section`, `record`, `metric_or_residue`, `value_or_count`, `frequency`, and `notes`. `section=summary` rows report scalar metrics such as sequence length and molecular weight. `section=composition` rows report residue counts and frequencies.

sections.title: Metric Model
sections.content: Input residues are normalized case-insensitively. Gap symbols are excluded from composition counts. Stop symbols `*` are counted in composition but excluded from `residue_length` and from molecular-weight estimation. Molecular weight uses the shared Epithema average residue masses with one water molecule added once per chain.

sections.title: Legacy Context
sections.content: This acceptance anchor keeps one historical-style `pepstats` reporting example in view and compares the Epithema tabular payload against a committed expected output. The comparison validates the governed first-release metric set rather than the broader historical EMBOSS biochemical-report surface.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema pepstats`. Validation currently covers protein summary rows, composition frequencies, stop-symbol handling, unsupported ambiguous-residue failure for mass estimation, and nucleotide-input rejection.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not estimate isoelectric point, extinction coefficient, or advanced residue-class summaries. Ambiguous protein symbols that are not supported by the shared molecular-weight helper, such as `X` or `Z`, cause the run to fail instead of being approximated.

artifacts.label: Protein statistics fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed protein fixture used for deterministic pepstats validation.

examples.title: Compute per-record protein statistics
examples.description: Reports scalar summary metrics and per-residue composition rows for a small protein fixture.

expected_outputs.label: Protein statistics table
expected_outputs.description: A stable table containing per-record summary metrics and amino-acid composition frequencies.
